name: Pork St. Tammany
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook rice according to package directions. Set aside.
2. Pour boiling water over apricots; let stand 20 minutes to soften. Drain.
3. In a large skillet, sauté green onions, mushrooms, and green pepper in butter until tender.
4. Stir in rice, apricots, pecans, parsley, and seasonings.
5. Remove strings from pork roast and slice it lengthwise (if not pre-sliced). Slice the pork pieces to just under one side but not through.
6. Lay the pork pieces side by side and spoon the stuffing mixture evenly over the pork.
7. Starting with one long side, roll the pork in a jellyroll fashion, tying securely at 2-inch intervals with heavy string.
8. Place the roast on a lightly greased rack in a shallow roasting pan. Arrange bacon lengthwise over the roast.
9. Insert a meat thermometer into the thickest part of the roast, ensuring it does not touch fat or stuffing.
10. Cover the roast with aluminum foil and bake at 325°F for 3 hours, or until the meat thermometer reads 160°F (30-35 minutes per pound).
11. Remove the foil for the last 30-40 minutes of baking.
12. Remove the roast from the oven and let it rest for 5 minutes before slicing. Garnish with apricot halves and parsley if desired.
Yield: 12 to 15 servings.
